steps.action: Navigate to Storyline tab in project setup wizard
steps.expected: Storyline tab renders with animation components
steps.observed: Storyline tab loaded successfully with Import/Upload section and storyline display area

steps.expected: Component implements word-by-word blur-in/typewriter animation using Framer Motion
steps.observed: StreamingTextAnimate.tsx (89 lines) implements progressive typewriter/blur-in with: initial={{ opacity: 0, filter: 'blur(8px)' }}, animate={{ opacity: 1, filter: 'blur(0px)' }}, staggered delay per word (0.04s), blinking cursor during streaming

steps.action: Verify StreamingTextAnimate is used in StorylineTab.tsx
steps.expected: Component imported and rendered for storyline text display
steps.observed: StorylineTab.tsx imports StreamingTextAnimate at line 13, renders it at line 461 for AI-generated text. Also uses TextAnimate (blurInUp, fadeIn by word) for section titles

steps.action: Verify unit tests exist for StreamingTextAnimate
steps.observed: StreamingTextAnimate.test.tsx exists with 6 tests covering: rendering text, empty text, isStreaming cursor, non-streaming, className prop, word splitting

evidence.codeInspection: StreamingTextAnimate.tsx uses Framer Motion motion.span with blur-in animation (blur 8px→0px, opacity 0→1), 0.04s stagger per word, blinking cursor during streaming. Imported and used in StorylineTab.tsx line 461. TextAnimate used for section titles with blurInUp and fadeIn by word.

steps.expected: Upload area visible with supported formats listed
steps.observed: Upload area displays 'Drag & drop a script, chapter, or treatment' with 'PDF, DOCX, MD, TXT — up to 10 MB' and Browse files button

steps.action: Upload test .txt file via file input
steps.expected: File parsed and content used as storyline input
steps.observed: Toast notification 'Document loaded' appeared confirming successful parse. StorylineDocumentUpload.tsx handles: file selection, drag-drop, loading state (spinner), error handling (toast), and URL import
'Import from URL' button visible below upload area, with URL input and Import button in expandable section

evidence.network: TXT file parsed client-side (no edge function needed). PDF/DOCX parsing via document-parse edge function.

steps.expected: Clone Voice button visible in VOICEOVER SELECTION section
steps.observed: Clone Voice button with + icon visible next to VOICEOVER SELECTION heading

steps.expected: Dialog opens with audio upload instructions and requirements
steps.observed: Dialog opens with: mic icon + 'Clone Voice' title, description about uploading clear audio, requirements box (Format: MP3/WAV, Duration: 30s-5min, Quality: clear speech), Voice Name input, drag-drop zone for audio files, Cancel and Clone Voice buttons

steps.action: Verify dialog has progress indicator support
steps.observed: VoiceCloneDialog.tsx implements Progress component with stages: idle→validating→uploading→success/error. Progress bar shows percentage (0-100%) with animated updates during upload. Success state shows CheckCircle2 icon.

evidence.network: Clone voice calls elevenlabs-voices edge function with action: 'clone', audioBase64, name

steps.observed: Dialog showing Format: MP3/WAV, Duration: 30s-5min requirements

steps.expected: Rejection with validation error message about minimum duration
steps.observed: Red error box displayed: 'Audio is too short (1s). Minimum duration is 30 seconds.' File was not accepted, drop zone remained empty.

steps.expected: File type, size, and duration validation before upload
steps.observed: validateAudioFile() checks: 1) file type (MP3/WAV), 2) file size (max 50MB), 3) audio duration via Web Audio API (min 30s, max 300s/5min). Validation errors show in red AlertCircle box. All validation happens client-side before upload.

evidence.codeInspection: VoiceCloneDialog.tsx validates: ACCEPTED_AUDIO_TYPES=['audio/mpeg','audio/wav','audio/x-wav','audio/wave'], MIN_DURATION_SECONDS=30, MAX_DURATION_SECONDS=300, MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB=50. Error messages include actual vs expected values.

assertions.title: Improved Cast Member Card UI

steps.expected: Cast section visible with Add character card
steps.observed: Cast section shows 'Add character' placeholder card and text 'No characters generated or added yet.'

steps.expected: Cleaner layout with avatar, hover overlay with Edit/Generate/Delete, state indicators
steps.observed: CharacterCard.tsx (264 lines) implements: 1) Avatar display area — shows character image, loading spinner during generation, or placeholder User icon in circular frame. 2) Hover overlay — gradient overlay (from-black/90 via-black/50) with 3 action buttons: Edit (Pencil icon, disabled without image), Generate/Regenerate (ImagePlus icon with primary color), Delete (Trash2 icon with red color). 3) Generation state badges — STATUS_BADGE map with 4 states: pending (Clock, zinc), generating (Loader2 spinning, primary), completed (CheckCircle2, green), failed (AlertCircle, red). 4) Info footer with name (truncated) and description. Uses Framer Motion for layout animations and AnimatePresence for image transitions.

steps.action: Verify CharacterCard is rendered in SettingsTab
steps.expected: CharacterCard imported and used for character display
steps.observed: SettingsTab.tsx imports CharacterCard at line 12, renders it at line 344 inside a character cards grid section

evidence.codeInspection: CharacterCard.tsx has: avatar display (image/placeholder), hover overlay with 3 action buttons (Edit/Generate/Delete), 4 generation state indicator badges (pending/generating/completed/failed), Framer Motion animations, Card-based layout with aspect-[3/4] proportion. Cannot test hover actions visually since no characters exist without a saved project.

assertions.issues: Character cards could not be fully tested in browser because creating a character requires a saved project (projectId), which this fresh wizard doesn't have. Card component is code-verified with all required features present.

frictions.description: Creating characters requires a saved project (projectId). In a fresh project-setup wizard without prior completion, the 'Add character' action shows 'Please save the project first' error. This prevents visual testing of CharacterCard hover overlay and state indicators.
frictions.resolution: Used code inspection of CharacterCard.tsx as supplemental evidence to verify all required UI features exist in the component

frictions.description: The intro animation plays on first visit to any page, requiring a click to dismiss before testing can begin.
frictions.resolution: Clicked to dismiss intro, then navigated to /home

frictions.description: Storyline generation (for testing typewriter animation live) requires API keys for the Groq/storyline model which are not available in the test environment.
frictions.resolution: Used code inspection to verify StreamingTextAnimate component implements the required word-by-word blur-in animation with Framer Motion

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. VAL-SETUP-009 (document upload) confirmed via successful .txt upload with 'Document loaded' toast. VAL-SETUP-010 (voice clone dialog) opens with correct requirements and audio upload area. VAL-SETUP-011 (validation) correctly rejects 1s audio with 'too short' error. VAL-SETUP-008 (typewriter animation) and VAL-SETUP-012 (cast card UI) verified via code inspection showing complete implementations with Framer Motion animations, generation state indicators, and hover action buttons.
